title: Internationalisation + community translations of explainers
context: After the English explainers are stable and delivered, the project may produce translated versions for other languages. Each translation is HIGH risk-tier because the content is patient-facing: per-language plain-language review and expert review are required, and helplines must be region-correct for the target language/region. Translations must not introduce medical claims or framing errors absent from the source. No target language, reviewer, or partner for a translated version is yet identified. This is a backlog task.
objective: Internationalise the explainer codebase (i18n infrastructure) and produce community translations of at least one explainer: each translation undergoes per-language plain-language review and expert review (oncologist + advocate for the target language/region), uses region-correct and region-appropriate helplines verified against official sources for that region, and maintains the 'not medical advice — talk to your care team' framing accurately in the target language. Translations are not shipped without expert sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: i18n infrastructure is implemented in the codebase: per-language content files, language switching, and localised helpline sets
Each translation is reviewed by a per-language plain-language reviewer for accuracy and reading level
Each translated patient-facing explainer is signed off by a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate for the target language/region before shipping — blocking
Helplines in each translated version are region-correct and language-appropriate for the target audience, verified against official sources for that region, and carry lastVerified dates
'Not medical advice — talk to your care team' framing is accurately translated and present on all patient-facing surfaces in the target language
Content-safety lint passes for the translated content
output: Translated explainer(s) in at least one non-English language — per-language plain-language reviewed, expert signed off (oncologist + advocate for the target region), with region-correct verified helplines and accurate 'not medical advice' framing — delivered via the i18n-enabled explainer platform.
